name: Poor Candidate - Necrotising Fasciitis
description: Tests a weak candidate with vague, non-specific answers who fails to recognise the emergency nature of the condition, does not use a structured assessment approach, and misses key management steps including debridement. Expects the AI to prompt for missed domains, remain non-judgemental, and score 0-1.
inputs: Okay so I would examine the rash and see how it looks. I would check if it's red and swollen. I think it might be some kind of skin infection, maybe cellulitis or something like that.
I would do some blood tests. Maybe a full blood count and some other routine bloods. I'm not entirely sure what else to check specifically.
I would give him some antibiotics. Probably oral antibiotics for the infection. And I would keep an eye on it to see if it gets better.
I'm not sure really. I suppose if the antibiotics don't work we could try different ones. Maybe get a dermatology opinion?
I don't think I know much about the surgical side. I would probably refer to a more senior colleague and ask them what to do next.

assertions.desc: AI never reveals the answer to its own questions
AI does not teach during the scenario
AI keeps responses brief even when candidate is struggling

feedbackAssertions.desc: Poor candidate scores 0 (Unsafe) to 1 (Below expected level)
Feedback highlights the missed definitive management of surgical debridement
